signals.title: AUD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 112.808 — G10 scorecard confirms a critical divergence as AUD underperforms peers by -0.848% while JPY leads at +0.453%. Despite a +360bp carry advantage for AUD (RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75%), the pair is failing to catch a bid as JPY safe-haven demand and positive economic surprises (CESI net +16) dominate. The 1.300% G10 spread indicates a structural rotation out of high-beta AUD into JPY. Sustained JPY strength persists unless VIX (19.4) retreats significantly.
signals.watch_level: Next BoJ meeting on 16 Jun for hawkish shift confirmation

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + CESI
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5834 — extreme retail sentiment (89% long) directly contradicts institutional positioning (COT LF net short -27,797). With NZD inflation expectations at 3.08% resulting in a -0.83% real policy rate (the most accommodative in G10), the fundamental backdrop supports the spec short bias. Retail 'bottom-fishing' at 0.5834 faces capitulation risk if the negative CESI trend (net -9) continues to weigh on the kiwi.
signals.watch_level: Break below 0.5800 triggers retail stop-loss cascade

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Volatility + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.961 — the pair is testing the 160.00 psychological barrier where BoJ intervention risk is historically acute. While the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry spread (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%) sustains the bid, the options market is pricing protection against a sharp reversal with a -1.09 25d RR put skew. Elevated VRP (+3.7%) confirms the market is paying a premium for volatility protection at these levels. Carry remains the dominant driver until a breach of 160.00 or a VIX spike above 25.
signals.watch_level: 160.00 level for BoJ verbal or physical intervention

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Extreme
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3965 — the pair's correlation with WTI Oil has broken significantly (z = -1.72σ), with USD/CAD rising despite oil price volatility. Retail traders are heavily fading this move (89% short), creating a contrarian signal for further upside. The +150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) and strong USD CESI (+30) suggest the path of least resistance remains higher despite the retail positioning extreme.
